channel.title: YouTube on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in YouTube on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Deleted Channel Content
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to show you some deleted content from this channel.

I’ve been doing this channel for more than two years now And, while it’s unscripted and I don’t edit anything that goes up, there has been enough deleted content, consisting mostly of failed intros that I didn’t continue with some bloopers, just some deleted content that I didn’t want to go with. And I thought you know what. This stuff never made it onto the channel until now. So enjoy. Hi everyone, it’s me, Tim. Welcome to a Insomnia edition of KON Games. Today, I’m going to talk about a design I made for an MMO set in the world of Arcanum, Arcanum — Arcanum that I called Arcanum MMO. Hi everyone, it’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk to you about my neverreleased Doctor Who game. Hi everyone, it’s me and Tim. Hi everyone, it’s me, Tim.

item.title: Channel Second Anniversary!
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about this Channel’s second anniversary.

It’s a fun Friday, it’s April 25th- 2025, and this Sunday, April 27th, is the 2year anniversary of this channel. I think I’ve mentioned. I thought I’d start it, do a few dozen videos, have a few hundred people watch them and be done. That hasn’t happened, so let’s walk through that, because it’s very surprising.

item.title: Channel Analytics (August 2024)
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Channel analytics.

Now, why am I talking about this? Well, I hit 160,000 Subs just a couple days ago — I’m filming this — at the end of August and a friend of mine saw me hit that and asked me about Channel analytics and I was like I don’t know what you’re referring to. He said: well, if you go into your YouTube, YouTu, Channel, you can dig around and pull up all these interesting numbers about your channel and I’m like, oh, that’s what that tab is for. I never looked at that tab. There’s like a, an earnings Tab and an analytics Tab and a copyright Tab and a um God. There’s a number of tabs that I either hardly look at or when I first started it, I opened them up and went huh, and then Clos them.

item.title: This Channel And The Game Industry
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about this Channel and the game industry, kind of a meta video, a video about my videos, kind of to put in perspective like what

I’ve been trying to do here for a year and a half. Um, and I’ll get to that, but before I get there I I need to tell a couple stories shocker. So My Generation — I think I hate the labels, but I would be the very oldest you can be and still be Gen X — my generation was the one that first really got into computers. I had a computer in high school. I was going online. I was really into technology.

item.title: Effect Of This Channel On Me
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the effect of this channel on me.

This has been. It was an interesting question. Um, I have another video about why a lot of people I know in the industry would never make a channel like this and you can go watch it. But this one was a response. This, video, is a response to a question I got asked by sherner, who asked: since you’ve started a YouTube channel, has it changed, your mind about any part of game development or reinforced your professional experiences? Yes, in fact, it’s done several of those, but I want to kick off the one that was the biggest surprise to me, which is a personal thing that had nothing to do with how I develop or how I think of my professional experiences, and that was very soon after making this channel. I realized that many people — not all — that’s the other thing.

item.title: Channel Memberships
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Channel memberships.

Yeah, I’m going to start Channel memberships on Canon games, and I know what you’re thinking. Why Channel memberships? Why? Now? I can answer that this channel has expanded Way Beyond where I thought it would be. I thought this channel would be me telling my stories to a few hundred, maybe a thousand of you.

Well, that’s not what happened. Currently — and this is, uh, late June of 2024 — currently I’m getting almost a million views a month and 100, almost 150,000 people have chosen to subscribe, so they’re getting the videos notifications, uh, when a new video comes up. I’m getting a lot of comments, several hundred a day, and a lot of those have questions in them that people want me to answer, and I’m trying my hardest. I’m keeping up reading all of them, but I can’t always answer all of them. I just don’t have the time, well, I’ve mentioned I do client work. One of my clients is winding. Down, the work for them is winding down, they’re.

item.title: My 100k Award
item.description:
Hi everyone, it’s me Tim, well, this thing just [Music].

Arrived. What

item.title: Why Aren't There More Channels Like This?
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about why there aren’t more game developers doing channels like this.

I’ve seen a lot of people ask or wonder in comments why there aren’t a lot more channels with people who’ve made games doing this and why I don’t have more people on this channel, and I’m going to break those up. I’m going to talk about why there aren’t channels like this. I’m also going to talk about why I haven’t done more chats because they’re related. And then I want to talk about kind of why it even took me 40 some years to make a to do something like this.

item.title: Making A YouTube Channel, Part 2
item.description:
Hi everyone, it’s me, Tim, and this is part two of making a YouTube channel.

So I did part one back in August, I think it was. I’ll put a link below, and when this video comes out it will have been 6 months since I started doing this. There’s over 180 videos on this channel now because I’ve done one a day. And I say when this comes out it’s because when I was doing the videos where every day I wore a different shirt out of my boxed collection of video game shirts, some of those shirts were kind of stinky, so I’d film a video and then I’d switch to another like one of my shirts that I’d washed, and sometimes that day I’d film another video. And that way I got about two weeks ahead, which really helped, because I took a week-long trip during the period of making this Channel and no one noticed, because there was no, No Interruption of videos, because I had that buffer.

item.title: Making A YouTube Channel
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to digress from Game Dev and just talk a little bit about what

I’ve learned in the last three months about trying to make a YouTube channel, in particular this channel. Um, so I started off. I just wanted to share some stories and technical details about game development. I’ve been doing it for four decades. Now I’m two years into my fifth decade and I thought I had a lot of interesting stories and Tech to tail, tech details that might even make sense to non-programmers. But my number one thing was I tried writing these stories and apparently I don’t get the tone right. So I thought, okay, I’ll do videos, and I looked around for a way of reaching as many people as possible, but without a paywall. I did not want to make people pay to get at these, so that landed me on YouTube, which I already had a subscription to because I keep all my music.

item.title: A Quick Note on Ads
item.description: So I just found out that some people are seeing ads in my videos. I did not think that ads would show up in unmonetized videos, but apparently they can be inserted. So what I’ve done is I turned on monetization, and immediately made all the ad skippable, and got rid of what are called mid-roll ads, which are ones that can show up in the middle of my videos. That’s the best I can do, except if I make videos that are, at least, it said, longer than eight minutes, but I tried it with some of my eight minute eight and a half minutes and it didn’t work. So it looks like it’s nine minutes or longer.

item.title: Introduction to "Cain on Games"!
item.description:
Hi there, my name is Timothy Kane and I’ve been a professional game developer for more than 40 years. Back in the 80s, I made a game called Grand Slam bridge and most recently I made an RPG called The Outer Worlds. In between, I made a lot of other games.

You may have heard Fallout. You may have enjoyed games from Troika games. I worked on MMOs for a while and even worked on a game that was one of the top grossing games from Kickstarter. I’m going to use this channel to tell stories about what game development has been like for the last 40 years and hopefully have some insights and some fun, so I hope you join me. Thank you, bye.
